---
title: "CARD_Walkthrough"
author: "Alos Diallo"
date: "2025-12-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
library(devtools)
BiocManager::install("TOAST")
devtools::install_github('xuranw/MuSiC')
devtools::install_github('YMa-lab/CARD')


```

1: In install.packages(...) :
  installation of package ‘RcppTOML’ had non-zero exit status
2: In install.packages(...) :
  installation of package ‘reticulate’ had non-zero exit status
3: In install.packages(...) :
  installation of package ‘basilisk’ ha

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(zellkonverter)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(CARD)

# --- STEP 1: LOAD REFERENCE (Your Atlas) ---
atlas <- readRDS("/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/All_Data/Visium_samples/Visium_Data/samples/atlas_data.rds")

# We need two things from the Atlas:
# 1. The Count Matrix (Raw counts preferred for deconvolution)
# 2. The Metadata (containing cell type labels)

# Check if 'counts' exists, otherwise use 'data'
sc_count <- GetAssayData(atlas, assay = "RNA", slot = "counts")

sc_meta <- atlas@meta.data

# --- STEP 2: LOAD SPATIAL DATA (From .h5ad) ---
# Direct load - no need for intermediate TIFFs or Seurat objects
sce <- readH5AD("/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/All_Data/Visium_samples/Visium_Data/samples/127.h5ad")

# Extract Spatial Counts
spatial_counts <- assay(sce, "X")

# Extract Coordinates (x and y) from the .h5ad metadata
# Note: Based on your previous script, we look in colData
spatial_coords <- as.data.frame(colData(sce)[, c("array_col", "array_row")])
colnames(spatial_coords) <- c("x", "y")

# Ensure coordinates are numeric
spatial_coords$x <- as.numeric(spatial_coords$x)
spatial_coords$y <- as.numeric(spatial_coords$y)

# --- STEP 3: RUN CARD ---

# Important: Update 'ct.varname' below to match the column in your atlas 
# that holds the cell types (e.g., "celltype", "annotation", or "cl295v11SubFull")

CARD_obj <- createCARDObject(
    sc_count = sc_count,
    sc_meta = sc_meta,
    spatial_count = spatial_counts,
    spatial_location = spatial_coords,
    ct.varname = "cl295v11SubFull", # I used the column name from your previous script
    ct.select = unique(sc_meta$cl295v11SubFull), 
    sample.varname = "orig.ident", 
    minCountGene = 100,
    minCountSpot = 5
) 

# Run the deconvolution
CARD_obj <- CARD_deconvolution(CARD_obj)

# --- STEP 4: VISUALIZE ---
# Visualize the proportion of a specific cell type
# Replace 'Tumor' with an actual cell type from your data
# CARD.visualize.prop(
#   proportion = CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD,
#   spatial_location = CARD_obj@spatial_location, 
#   ct.visualize = "Tumor", 
#   colors = c("lightblue","red"), 
#   pointSize = 3
# )
```

```{r}
# Extract the results matrix (Rows = Spots, Cols = Cell Types)
prop_matrix <- CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD

# Automatically find all columns containing "CD8"
cd8_cols <- grep("CD8", colnames(prop_matrix), value = TRUE)

# Print them to verify (Comparing against your list)
print(cd8_cols)
# Should catch: cTNI11, cTNI10, cTNI13, cTNI16, cTNI14, cTNI12, cTNI15
```

```{r}
# Create a copy of the results
broad_prop_matrix <- prop_matrix

# 1. Calculate Total CD8 by summing the specific columns row-wise
broad_prop_matrix <- as.data.frame(broad_prop_matrix)
broad_prop_matrix$CD8_Total <- rowSums(broad_prop_matrix[, cd8_cols])

# 2. (Optional) Remove the old fine-grained columns if you want a clean matrix
# broad_prop_matrix <- broad_prop_matrix[, !colnames(broad_prop_matrix) %in% cd8_cols]
```


```{r}
# Extract the new consolidated vector
cd8_proportions <- broad_prop_matrix$CD8_Total

# 1. Define the CD8 columns (if not already in memory)
# This grabs every column with "CD8" in the name
cd8_cols <- grep("CD8", colnames(CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD), value = TRUE)

# 2. Calculate the Sum
# We use drop=FALSE to ensure row names are preserved
cd8_sum <- rowSums(CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD[, cd8_cols])

# 3. Add this new "CD8_Total" column back to the CARD object's matrix
# This method ensures the new column has the correct Spot IDs
CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD <- cbind(CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD, CD8_Total = cd8_sum)

# 4. Visualize
# Now we can ask CARD to visualize "CD8_Total" by name, which is safer
plot_data <- cbind(CARD_obj@spatial_location, 
                   CD8_Total = CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD[, "CD8_Total"])

# 2. Plot with ggplot2
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = CD8_Total)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("lightblue", "red")) +
  theme_void() + # Removes axes and background for a cleaner spatial look
  labs(title = "Total CD8+ T-Cell Proportion", color = "Proportion") +
  coord_fixed() # Ensures the tissue shape isn't distorted



```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Prepare CARD Data
# We extract the specific column for CD8_Total
card_values <- CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD[, "CD8_Total"]

# 2. Input HiTIMED result for Sample 127
# Converting percentage (2.32) to proportion (0.0232) to match CARD
hitimed_pct <- 2.329332851
hitimed_prop <- hitimed_pct / 100 

# 3. Create Plotting Data
plot_df <- data.frame(
  Proportion = card_values,
  Method = "CARD (Spatial)"
)

# 4. Generate the Comparison Boxplot
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Method, y = Proportion)) +
  # A. The Boxplot (Spatial Distribution)
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#E69F00", alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +
  
  # B. The Jitter Points (Individual Spots)
  # This shows if the high average is driven by a few "hotspots" or general over-estimation
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.3, color = "#333333") +
  
  # C. The HiTIMED Reference Line
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = hitimed_prop, linetype = "HiTIMED (Methylation)"), 
             color = "blue", size = 1.2) +
  
  # D. Formatting and Labels
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, max(card_values) + 0.05)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "Bulk Reference", values = 2, 
                        guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "blue"))) +
  labs(
    title = "CD8+ T-Cell Estimation: RNA vs. DNA",
    subtitle = paste0("Sample 127\nHiTIMED: ", round(hitimed_pct, 2), "% | CARD Mean: ", round(mean(card_values)*100, 2), "%"),
    y = "Proportion of Cell Type",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# 5. Calculate Fold Difference for your records
fold_diff <- mean(card_values) / hitimed_prop
cat("Fold Difference (Spatial / Methylation):", round(fold_diff, 2), "x\n")
```
```{r}
# 1. Total Number of Spots in the Boxplot (N)
n_spots <- nrow(CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD)

# 2. Exact Statistics for the Boxplot
stats <- summary(CARD_obj@Proportion_CARD[, "CD8_Total"])

# 3. Print the report for your P.I.
cat("=== Boxplot Data Report ===\n")
cat("Total Spots Analyzed (N):", n_spots, "\n")
cat("Minimum Proportion:", round(stats["Min."]*100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Median Proportion:", round(stats["Median"]*100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Mean Proportion:", round(stats["Mean"]*100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Max Proportion:", round(stats["Max."]*100, 2), "%\n")
```
